title: tests: fix diff cmd test on macOS HFS+, fixes #8860
body:
This patch tightens the macOS-specific timing in the diff command tests. On macOS with HFS+ timestamp granularity, timestamp updates can be delayed when a file is touched shortly after creation. The test_time_diffs in the diff_cmd_test.py file currently accounts for a 1-second granularity but can still race if operations happen too quickly. The change adds more robust sleep durations for macOS (Darwin) to ensure mtime/ctime updates are observed, reducing flaky test failures on macOS HFS+.

No production code changes are required; the change is isolated to the test suite to stabilize behavior across platforms.

step_plan.summary: This change tightens macOS-specific timing in the diff command tests to stabilize test_time_diffs on HFS+ by extending sleep durations. No production code changes are introduced.

steps.operation: Extend Darwin-specific sleep durations in test_time_diffs to account for macOS HFS+ timestamp granularity
steps.reason: macOS HFS+ timestamp granularity can delay mtime/ctime updates; longer sleeps ensure file modifications are observed and reduce flaky behavior on macOS
steps.side_effects: Increases test runtime modestly on macOS; no production code changes; limits changes to Darwin-specific path in the test; unrelated platforms remain unaffected
